source_alignment.subject: Science
source_alignment.source_statement: Describe the movement of the Earth and other planets relative to the sun in the solar system, and describe the movement of the moon relative to the Earth.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This pack teaches Earth, Sun and Moon movement using safe scaled models and orbit simulations.

objective.child_goal: I can use a model to explain Earth, Sun and Moon movement.
objective.statement: Describe Earth, Sun and Moon movement using scientific models and explain day, year and lunar orbit ideas.
misconceptions: Thinks the Sun moves around the Earth each day.
Thinks day and night happen because clouds cover the Sun.
Thinks the Moon makes its own light.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The path one object takes around another object in space.
vocabulary.audio_script: An orbit is a path around another object.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A glowing path around Earth or the Sun.

vocabulary.audio_script: Earth rotates. That means it spins.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Earth spinning on a tilted axis.

vocabulary.child_explanation: To bounce light from one surface to another.
vocabulary.audio_script: The Moon reflects light from the Sun.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Sunlight arrow bouncing from Moon to Earth.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Match rotate and orbit to the correct movement.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Rotate means spin. Orbit means move around something.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Two motion cards compare spin and orbit.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Use the observatory model to test what moves around what.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce model-based reasoning for space movement.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: A model helps us explain movement that is too big to see all at once.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: An orbit table with Sun, Earth and Moon pieces.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Earth rotates, so one side faces the Sun and the other side has night.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Explain day and night using rotation, not Sun orbiting Earth.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Day and night happen because Earth spins.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Earth rotates with a lit side and dark side.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Drag Earth onto its orbit around the Sun.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Practise relative movement of Earth and Sun.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Earth travels around the Sun on its orbit.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Orbit path with draggable Earth model.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Does the Moon make its own light?
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The Moon does not make its own light. It reflects sunlight.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Sunlight reflects from Moon toward Earth.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach Sol why day and night do not happen because the Sun moves around Earth.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Use teach-back to repair geocentric day/night reasoning.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: I thought the Sun goes around us every day. Can you show me the model?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The companion watches the Earth rotation model.

manipulatives.purpose: Let learners manipulate Earth, Sun and Moon models to explain relative movement.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners rotate Earth, build orbit paths, compare models and run reflection tests.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Reduced-motion uses step frames; audio labels explain each movement and light path.

manipulatives.purpose: Build scientific model literacy by judging which Earth-space diagrams explain day and night, a year and the Moon's orbit, and where each model has limits.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners classify annotated models by the motion they can explain, flag misleading scale or viewpoint features and select evidence that distinguishes rotation from orbit.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Diagrams include concise text and audio descriptions with shape-coded bodies; keyboard commands, no-drag choice buttons and single-switch scanning support all sorting, while static and reduced-motion modes present numbered before-and-after frames with no spinning or orbit animation.

practice.interleaving_notes: Interleave day/night, Earth orbit and Moon reflection only after rotation/orbit vocabulary is secure.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Distinguish rotation from orbit using animated or static model cards.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: movement clip/card + rotate/orbit choice + label feedback
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Use reduced-motion alternatives for all moving diagrams.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Explain day and night with Earth rotation.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: Earth model + Sun light source + rotate control
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Avoid implying the model is to scale unless labelled as not to scale.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Build correct relative movement paths for Earth and Moon.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: drag Earth/Moon to orbit paths + predict motion
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep scale caveats visible to teachers and older pupils.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Explain the Moon as reflecting sunlight rather than making light.
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Do not overteach moon phases in this pack.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Maintain movement vocabulary and model reasoning through spaced review.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: short model prompt + one movement/evidence decision

body.prompt: What causes day and night on Earth?
choices: The Moon blocks the Sun every night

hints: One side faces the Sun while the other is dark.
question_variants.explanation: Day and night happen because Earth rotates.

body.prompt: Why can we see the Moon?
choices: It reflects light from the Sun
It makes all its own light

expected_answer.value: It reflects light from the Sun

question_variants.explanation: We see the Moon because it reflects sunlight.
Rotate means spin around an axis.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Thinks the Sun moves around the Earth each day.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner chooses Sun orbit as day/night explanation.
repair_sequence: Hold the Sun fixed in the model.
Label day side and night side.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner explains day/night using Earth rotation.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Thinks the Moon makes its own light.
repair_sequence: Ask learner to choose reflect explanation.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner explains Moon visibility as reflected sunlight.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use calm dark-space colours and avoid rapid spinning.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Use step-through orbit frames and static light rays.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Narrate rotate, orbit and reflect actions with replay.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Use labelled model cards and short prompts.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Expose one model control at a time.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow freeze-frame inspection of movement paths.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach Sun, Earth, Moon, orbit, rotate, reflect, day and night.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires describing Earth/Sun/Moon movement; secure requires explaining day/night and Moon reflection using model evidence.
evidence.parent_summary: Can use a model to explain Earth, Sun and Moon movement, including day and night.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Distinguishes rotation and orbit, builds relative movement models and explains reflected moonlight.
next_actions: Connect Earth orbit to year length.
Add moon phase extension after reflection is secure.

qa.notes: Sample Year 5 Earth/space pack using model-based simulation.
